thesis: A high-torque silver producer where stronger free cash flow and a broader operating base could convert commodity upside into disproportionate equity returns.

whyItMatters.label: Silver leverage
whyItMatters.detail: Equity sensitivity rises quickly when silver expands above the cost base.

whyItMatters.label: Reserve base
whyItMatters.detail: Placeholder reserve-life estimate pending technical-report mapping.

whyItMatters.label: Jurisdiction quality
whyItMatters.detail: Established mining regions offset by Mexico policy and tax complexity.

whyItMatters.label: Growth potential
whyItMatters.detail: Mine optimization, portfolio integration, and restart optionality.

assets.name: San Dimas
assets.description: Core underground silver-gold operation and a major source of operating leverage.

assets.name: Santa Elena
assets.description: Underground operation with exploration and processing optimization potential.

assets.name: La Encantada
assets.description: Silver-focused operation where throughput and grade discipline drive margins.

assets.name: Jerritt Canyon
assets.description: Gold restart optionality with meaningful execution and capital requirements.

production.label: Silver equivalent
production.detail: Placeholder forward range for presentation design.

production.label: YoY growth
production.detail: Illustrative growth case, not company guidance.

scenarios.name: Bull case
scenarios.headline: Silver breaks higher while operating execution compounds the move.
points: Silver sustains a structurally higher price regime.
Core mines deliver stronger grades and lower unit costs.
Portfolio integration expands production and mine life.
Restart optionality gains strategic value without stressing the balance sheet.

scenarios.name: Base case
scenarios.headline: Cash generation improves, but valuation remains tied to execution.
points: Production meets the mid-range operating plan.
Free cash flow funds measured growth and debt reduction.
Multiple expansion is gradual rather than immediate.

scenarios.name: Bear case
scenarios.headline: Cost inflation and softer silver expose the operating leverage.
points: Silver retraces while energy and labor costs stay elevated.
Grades or recoveries underperform at a core operation.
Mexico tax or regulatory friction absorbs management attention.
Growth capital competes with balance-sheet repair.

financials.label: Market cap
financials.detail: Placeholder presentation value; map to daily market cache.

financials.label: Enterprise value
financials.detail: Placeholder; replace with normalized direct-source EV.

rickRule.label: Asset quality
rickRule.detail: Scale, mine life, and operating relevance.

rickRule.label: Valuation
rickRule.detail: Margin of safety versus normalized silver assumptions.

ericSprott.label: Torque score
ericSprott.detail: Sensitivity to a rising silver price.

timeline.title: First producing asset
timeline.description: The company begins its transition from exploration concept to operating silver producer.

timeline.title: San Dimas acquisition
timeline.description: A transformational transaction adds a cornerstone underground silver-gold operation.

timeline.title: Jerritt Canyon acquired
timeline.description: Nevada gold exposure adds strategic processing infrastructure and restart optionality.

timeline.title: Portfolio expansion
timeline.description: Illustrative integration milestone broadens the production and reserve platform.

timeline.title: Cash flow inflection
timeline.description: Higher silver prices, mine optimization, and disciplined capital allocation could reshape returns.

risks.name: Political
risks.mitigation: Diversification, active tax management, and disciplined capital deployment.

risks.name: Operational
risks.mitigation: Multiple producing assets, development discipline, and grade-control investment.

risks.name: Commodity price
risks.mitigation: Liquidity, cost control, and staged growth capital.

risks.name: Balance sheet
risks.mitigation: Free-cash-flow retention and measured restart spending.
